round1: Round 1 is mercifully over, and it seems the upsets were a bit much for some of you. While Stephanie pulled off an impressive feat by picking every single underdog to win, M.C.B. appears to have consulted a Magic 8-Ball that only said "Outlook Not So Good" for all the right choices.
round2: Round 2 is in the books, and it seems most of you still don't understand how hockey works. While M.C.B. and Jaclyn actually paid attention to the Detroit series and called it perfectly, the rest of you collectively decided Edmonton was a good pick, which aged like milk, and Mom and M.C.B. are still wondering where Montreal went.
round3: Round 3 saw Detroit and Washington advance, both in 6 games. Marc and Jaclyn actually managed to pick a winner, nailing Detroit in 6, while Dad and Stephanie spectacularly missed both series. Solid effort, folks.
round4: Well, that was a bust. Every single one of you picked Washington, and every single one of you got burned as Detroit swept the series. Maybe next year you'll learn to pick a winner.
overall: Alright, the 1998 NHL playoffs are in the books, and while Detroit hoisted the actual Cup, Jaclyn single-handedly owned this pool, pulling off a dominant performance with 42 points that left everyone else eating dust. Chrissy, on the other hand, somehow managed to pick nine teams and still couldn't break 25 points, securing her spot as this year's undisputed pool loser – a truly pathetic display, though Stephanie's ten-team, three-correct-games-score disaster deserves an honorable mention for sheer futility. Marc and Derrick put up a respectable fight, but ultimately just watched Jaclyn skate away with the bragging rights and whatever meager prize was on the line. Some people clearly pay attention to hockey, and others just pick names out of a hat.
